description_long: This is a military-style attack helicopter, resembling the AH-64 Apache, with a streamlined, angular fuselage and a tandem cockpit. The helicopter features a prominent main rotor with four long, narrow blades and a smaller tail rotor mounted vertically at the end of a slender tail boom. Its exterior is finished in a matte, dark olive drab color, with visible hardpoints for armaments and sensors mounted on the nose and sides. The landing gear consists of robust, fixed skids designed for stability on rough terrain. The cockpit windows are faceted and tinted, providing wide visibility for the crew. Various antennas, sensors, and exhausts are visible along the fuselage, contributing to its tactical appearance.
description: A dark olive, military-style attack helicopter with a four-blade main rotor, tandem cockpit, and fixed landing skids. Features include faceted tinted windows, visible armaments, and a vertical tail rotor.
description_view_0: Front view showing the narrow profile, tandem cockpit, and the full span of the main rotor blades.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the elongated fuselage, tail boom, and the vertical tail rotor at the rear.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail rotor, tail boom, and the symmetrical arrangement of the landing skids.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the main rotor, cockpit, and external equipment mounted on the fuselage.
attribute_rotor_blades: Four long, narrow main rotor blades, likely made of composite material, colored dark gray or black with no visible markings. The blades are straight and evenly spaced.
attribute_tail_rotor: A vertical tail rotor with four short blades, mounted at the end of the tail boom. The blades are dark in color and appear to be made of composite material, with no visible markings or protective shrouds.
attribute_landing_skids: Fixed, robust landing skids made of metal, colored dark gray or black, with visible crossbars and support struts for stability.
attribute_cockpit_windows: Multiple faceted, angular cockpit windows arranged in a tandem configuration. The windows are large, tinted, and framed with dark material, providing wide visibility for both crew members.
